title: class2_notification_payload_schema
description: Authoritative schema for caregiver escalation and Class 2 clarification notification payloads emitted when the system enters or remains in a Class 2 clarification/escalation path. This schema governs outbound Class 2 notification contracts and should not be overridden by companion output-profile assets. For doorlock-sensitive or other sensitive actuation cases, this payload may describe escalation and a governed manual confirmation path, but it must not itself authorize actuation or bypass validator, caregiver approval, ACK, or audit boundaries. Version 1.1.0 extends v1.0.0 for Class 2 clarification/transition semantics and C206/C207 trigger IDs.

event_summary.description: Short summary of the triggering event or clarification state.

context_summary.description: Short summary of the relevant environmental, user-input, device-state, and unresolved-context information.

unresolved_reason.description: Human-readable reason why autonomous handling was not completed or why clarification/caregiver confirmation is required.

manual_confirmation_path.description: Instruction or route by which a caregiver can review, confirm, deny, or intervene. This field may describe a governed manual confirmation path, but does not by itself authorize autonomous low-risk execution or sensitive actuation. For sensitive actuation such as doorlock control, this field describes a review route only and does not create execution authority.

audit_correlation_id.description: Correlation ID linking the escalation or clarification payload to audit logs and upstream events.

notification_channel.description: Outbound channel used to deliver the escalation or clarification notification payload.

source_layer.description: System layer that initiated the Class 2 escalation or clarification notification.

exception_trigger_id.description: Canonical Class 2 trigger identifier. C206 and C207 support Class 2 clarification/transition semantics introduced in policy v1.2.0. C208 covers visitor/doorbell-detected doorlock-sensitive paths added in the C208 routing addendum.
